payload.title: Which repo conventions should Scribe treat as canonical?
Which files or paths should Builder avoid by default?
What Git workflow should Git-master assume for this project?
Tower role leakage into direct implementation if delegation discipline is not maintained.
Memory drift if imported sessions are not curated into docs and working summaries.
Session ambiguity if Tower sessions are not resumed through `tower resume`.

Compared the current homepage against the upstream conference template and found that the largest alignment gaps are the hero/banner treatment, the navigation rhythm, and several homepage-only CSS customizations that replace the template’s plain content flow with a more custom landing-page look. The minimum Builder change set is to restore the template’s image-backed banner and navigation proportions, then keep the ATLAS copy while removing the custom callout styling rather than rewriting content.
.control-tower/packets/inbox/scout-homepage-gap-result.json
Homepage Template Gap Analysis
.control-tower/packets/outbox/scout-homepage-gap.json

Current ATLAS homepage preserves the upstream template’s fixed-width body, table-based navigation, and simple single-file delivery, but it materially diverges in three places: the banner system was replaced with a custom gradient panel and one-line title treatment, the homepage information architecture was converted from the template’s page-route navigation into anchored in-page sections, and several custom content presentation styles were added (subtitle block, quote/question callouts, bulleted scope list). The minimum Builder change set to restore template alignment while preserving ATLAS content is to reintroduce the template banner/title composition and navigation proportions, while keeping the single-page sections and existing workshop copy.
.control-tower/packets/inbox/scout-ea3e085b-a74e-4471-bdf0-bd4d488e0fd0-result.json
Single-page anchor navigation is an intentional product-level deviation from the upstream conference template and should not be reverted without a docs/spec change.
